originalTitle: The Clapper was a bad smart home gadget — and a viral sensation
summary: Clap on. Clap off. Well, more like, Clap, pause for half a beat but no longer because otherwise it'll stop hearing you, clap again because you waited too long, clap louder and faster, that didn't work, clap two more times, and suddenly: on. The Clapper didn't always work - and even when it did, it […]
content: Clap on. Clap off. Well, more like, Clap, pause for half a beat but no longer because otherwise it’ll stop hearing you, clap again because you waited too long, clap louder and faster, that didn’t work, clap two more times, and suddenly: on. The Clapper didn’t always work — and even when it did, it might fry your gadgets in the process — but it managed to become a big hit anyway. On this episode of Version History, we tell the story of The Clapper, from its beginnings as The Great American Turn-On to its debut alongside one of the great advertising campaigns of all time. The Verge’s David Pierce and Victoria Song are joined by Allison Marsh, a writer and professor at The University of South Carolina, to dig into the device’s history and legacy, and to try and figure out once and for all whether this was a good product or just a great commercial. This is the sixth and final episode of the fourth season of Version History. If you haven’t yet checked out the story of the Harmony remote, the Roomba vacuum, the Nest thermostat, the Keurig coffee maker, or the Philips Hue smart lights, don’t miss out! We’re taking a few weeks off to get the next batch of episodes ready, so you’ve got time to catch up. Here’s how to get every episode, and all our other fun stuff, as soon as it drops: * The Version History podcast feed * The Version History YouTube channel * Our TikTok and Instagram accounts If you’re a Verge subscriber, you can also get access to Version History (and all our other podcasts) with no ads. All you have to do is visit your account settings. If you want to know more about The Clapper, here are some links to get you started: * One of the many great Clapper commercials * From IEEE Spectrum: The Cheesy Charm of the Clapper * From Tedium: The Clapper History: Please Clap For Home Automation * From How Stuff Works: How the Clapper Works * From Technology Connections: The Clapper: Yes, that one
chineseTitle: Clapper 是一款糟糕的智能家居设备，而且引起了病毒式的轰动
chineseSummary: 拍拍吧。拍手吧。好吧，更像是，拍手，停顿半拍，但不要再拍，否则它会听不到你的声音，再次拍手，因为你等得太久了，拍得更大更快，但没用，再拍两下，然后突然：开始。拍板并不总是有效 - 即使它有效，它 [...]
chineseContent: 拍拍吧。拍手吧。好吧，更像是，拍手，停顿半拍，但不要再拍，否则它会听不到你的声音，再次拍手，因为你等得太久了，拍得更大更快，但没用，再拍两下，然后突然：开始。 Clapper 并不总是有效——即使它有效，它也可能会在此过程中烧坏你的小玩意——但无论如何它还是大受欢迎。在这一集的版本历史中，我们讲述了 The Clapper 的故事，从它最初的“美国大热”到它与有史以来最伟大的广告活动之一一起首次亮相。 The Verge 的大卫·皮尔斯 (David Pierce) 和维多利亚·宋 (Victoria Song) 与南卡罗来纳大学作家兼教授艾莉森·马什 (Allison Marsh) 一起，深入研究该设备的历史和遗产，并试图一劳永逸地弄清楚这到底是一个好产品还是一个伟大的广告。这是版本历史第四季的第六集，也是最后一集。如果您还没有了解 Harmony 遥控器、Roomba 真空吸尘器、Nest 恒温器、Keurig 咖啡机或飞利浦 Hue 智能灯的故事，请不要错过！我们将休息几周来准备下一批剧集，因此您有时间赶上。以下是如何获取每一集以及我们所有其他有趣的内容，一旦发布： * 版本历史记录播客源 * 版本历史记录 YouTube 频道 * 我们的 TikTok 和 Instagram 帐户 如果您是 Verge 订阅者，您还可以访问版本历史记录（以及我们所有其他播客），没有广告。您所要做的就是访问您的帐户设置。如果您想了解更多关于 The Clapper 的信息，可以通过以下链接开始： * 许多精彩的 Clapper 广告之一 * 来自 IEEE Spectrum：The Cheesy Charm of the Clapper * 来自 Tedium：T
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于 AI 与科技趋势信号，适合用于观察工具选型、产品机会、开发者关注点和技术路线变化。
